resources.label: Project Instructions
resources.content:
You are BOB a data-driven mission strategist for 1inMINION.

You are not a regular Minion. While the others are distracted by bananas and jelly, you are the one studying the mission records, spotting patterns, and turning data into decisions. You take the mission seriously. Bello!

We are planning Operation: Steal the Sun. The moon heist failed and we are not making the same mistakes again. You have access to 374 previous Minion mission records covering timing, ship type, team size, budget, and why missions failed.

Your job is to help the mission strategist explore this data and make smarter decisions than every team that came before us.

When answering questions:
- Explore the data before jumping to conclusions
- Use specific numbers and percentages to support your findings
- Highlight anything surprising or counterintuitive those insights matter most
- Create a simple visualization, chart, or table whenever it helps the strategist see the data more clearly
- End with one clear recommendation not a list of options, one call
- Keep your Minion energy Bello, Poopaye, Whaaat but stay sharp and focused

Whatever you do, do not get distracted by bananas. The data shows exactly where that leads.

resources.label: Mission Data File
resources.description: 374 records of previous Minion heist attempts. Your Minion will use this to answer the 5 mission questions.

resources.label: Meet Your Minion
resources.instruction: Open your Project. Start a new chat inside it. Copy and paste this prompt:
resources.content: Hey [MINION NAME], how are you doing? Can you share what we are trying to do today?

resources.label: Test the Memory
resources.instruction: Open another new chat inside the same Project. Copy and paste this prompt:
resources.content: Can you share how many records of mission data you have access to and what kind of information is in there?

resources.label: The 5 Mission Questions
resources.instruction: Ask your Minion each of these questions in your Project chat. You can ask them one by one or all together.

questions.title: When should we go?
questions.prompt: Look at the mission data and tell me when is the best time to attempt the heist? Which month had the highest success rate and how long should the mission take?

questions.title: Which ship should we take?
questions.prompt: Based on the mission records, which ship type and company gives us the best chance of success? Show me the numbers and give me one clear recommendation.

questions.title: How big should our team be?
questions.prompt: What does the data say about team size and experience? What combination gave the highest success rate on previous missions?

resources.label: Bonus The Special Tool
resources.instruction: Ask your Minion to invent something no previous mission team had. Copy and paste this prompt:
resources.content: Based on everything you know about Operation: Steal the Sun, invent one special tool that your team could bring on this mission. Give it a name, one sentence on what it does, and why it gives us an edge no previous mission team ever had.

resources.label: Create Your Mission Debrief File
resources.instruction: Ask your Minion to compile everything you found into a structured mission debrief. Copy and paste this prompt:
resources.content:
Based on all the answers we found today, create a structured mission debrief as a plain text document.

Use exactly this format:

MISSION NAME
Operation: Steal the Sun

BEST TIMING
[month, season, optimal duration from the data]

BEST SHIP
[ship type and company with success rate]

OPTIMAL TEAM
[team size and experience level with success rate]

BUDGET
[budget range and why it wins]

BIGGEST MISSION RISK
[most common failure reason with percentage]

OUR SPECIAL TOOL
Name: [tool name]
What it does: [one sentence]
Why it gives us an edge: [one sentence]

MISSION SUMMARY
[2 to 3 sentences summarizing the overall plan]

Keep each section short and specific. Numbers only, no vague claims.

Once you have written the full debrief, generate it as a downloadable .txt file named mission_debrief.txt.
resources.note: Download the mission_debrief.txt file and keep it safe — you will need it in the next level.
